New York City Street Tree Census — EDA & Data Cleaning
Project Overview
This project demonstrates Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and data cleaning techniques using the 2015 New York City Street Tree Census dataset. The dataset contains detailed information about street trees across New York City boroughs, including:

  • Tree species
  • Health condition
  • Trunk diameter
  • Geographic location
  • Sidewalk damage
  • Tree-related problems\

Objectives
The main objectives of this project are:

  • Perform Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
  • Clean and prepare the dataset for analysis
  • Identify missing values, inconsistencies, and duplicates
  • Explore patterns and relationships within the data
  • Create visualizations to better understand tree characteristics and health conditions

Dataset Source
The dataset can be found here:
https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Environment/2015-Street-Tree-Census-Tree-Data/uvpi-gqnh/data_preview

# 'health', 'spc_latin', 'spc_common' and 'sidewalk'  have almost the same percent of nulls: 4.6% , there must be a relationship on those columns.  

print(df['health'].isna().sum())
print(df['sidewalk'].isna().sum())
print(df['spc_common'].isna().sum())
print(df['spc_latin'].isna().sum())

31616
31616

In [129]:
# Pri
print("Alive:")
print("health  sidewalk  spc_common  spc_latin")
print(
        df[df['status'] == 'Alive'] ['health'].isna().sum(), "      ",
        df[df['status'] == 'Alive'] ['sidewalk'].isna().sum(),"       ",
        df[df['status'] == 'Alive'] ['spc_common'].isna().sum(),"         ",
        df[df['status'] == 'Alive'] ['spc_latin'].isna().sum()
)



print("Dead/Stump:")
print("health  sidewalk  spc_common  spc_latin")
print(
       df[df['status'].isin(['Stump', 'Dead'])]['health'].isna().sum(),"  ",
       df[df['status'].isin(['Stump', 'Dead'])]['sidewalk'].isna().sum(),"   ",
       df[df['status'].isin(['Stump', 'Dead'])]['spc_common'].isna().sum(),"    ",
       df[df['status'].isin(['Stump', 'Dead'])]['spc_latin'].isna().sum()
)

health  sidewalk  spc_common  spc_latin

health  sidewalk  spc_common  spc_latin
31615    31615     31614      31614

The majority of nulls values in the columns 'health', 'spc_latin', 'spc_common' and 'sidewalk' is when the status of the tree is Dead or Stump.

In [130]:
# This will print the rows where health is null and status is different than 'Stump' and 'dead'

print("Health \n", df[(df['health'].isna()) & (~df['status'].isin(['Stump','Dead']))] [['health','status', 'sidewalk','spc_common','spc_latin']])
print("sidewalk \n", df[(df['sidewalk'].isna()) & (~df['status'].isin(['Stump','Dead']))] [['health','status', 'sidewalk','spc_common','spc_latin']])
print("spc_common \n", df[(df['spc_common'].isna()) & (~df['status'].isin(['Stump','Dead']))] [['health','status', 'sidewalk','spc_common','spc_latin']])
print("spc_latin \n", df[(df['spc_latin'].isna()) & (~df['status'].isin(['Stump','Dead']))] [['health','status', 'sidewalk','spc_common','spc_latin']])

In [131]:
# Now let's check the column 'problems' wichs shows about 67% of nulls 

print(df['problems'].isna().sum())

457944
In [132]:
# It is suspected that column problems has a null value when the columns below
# are all of them equal to 'No', the following code is written to prove that 
# theory.

problems_cols = ['root_stone','root_grate', 'root_other','trunk_wire',
                'trnk_light','trnk_other', 'brch_light', 'brch_shoe', 
                'brch_other']

df['has_a_problem'] = df[problems_cols].eq('Yes').any(axis=1)

print(df['has_a_problem'].value_counts())

df[(df['has_a_problem']) & (df['problems'].isna())]

has_a_problem
False    457944
True     225844
Name: count, dtype: int64
Out[132]:
tree_id block_id created_at tree_dbh stump_diam curb_loc status health spc_latin spc_common steward guards sidewalk user_type problems root_stone root_grate root_other trunk_wire trnk_light trnk_other brch_light brch_shoe brch_other address postcode zip_city community board borocode borough cncldist st_assem st_senate nta nta_name boro_ct state latitude longitude x_sp y_sp council district census tract bin bbl has_a_problem

No rows are printed. This confirms the fact that the nulls in problems is when all the columns above 'root_stone','root_grate', 'root_other','trunk_wire', 'trnk_light','trnk_other', 'brch_light', 'brch_shoe', 'brch_other' are equal to No
We can replace the values null of problems with the text: "No problems"

In [135]:
# Insert a new column that will contain the diameter of the tree trunk in centimeters

# Coversion from inches to centimeters 
df['tree_dbh_cm'] = df['tree_dbh'] * 2.54

# Since the new column is inserted at the end, the following procedure will move the last column and put it after to the tree_dbh column

cols = list(df.columns)

cols.remove('tree_dbh_cm')

idx = cols.index('tree_dbh')

cols.insert(idx + 1,'tree_dbh_cm')

df = df[cols]

df.head()

Cleaning¶

In [138]:
# First we will fix the NaN values of the columns "problems"

condition = df[problems_cols].eq('No').all(axis=1)
df.loc[condition & df['problems'].isna(), 'problems'] = 'No problems'
In [139]:
# Convert the column of corrdinates from str to numeric.

df['latitude'] = df['latitude'].str.replace(',','.',regex=False)
df['longitude'] = df['longitude'].str.replace(',','.',regex=False)

df['latitude'] = pd.to_numeric(df['latitude'], errors='coerce')
df['longitude'] = pd.to_numeric(df['longitude'], errors ='coerce')
In [140]:
#  Remove the rows with values null for the columns: 'health', 'spc_latin', 'spc_common' and 'sidewalk' where status = 'Alive'

rows_to_remove = [32889, 346299, 356613, 427541, 431417, 608632, 656960]

df = df.drop(index=rows_to_remove)
In [141]:
# We will work only with Trees in status "Alive"

df_clean = df[df['status'] == 'Alive']
In [142]:
print(df_clean.shape)
print(df_clean.columns)

(652166, 47)
Index(['tree_id', 'block_id', 'created_at', 'tree_dbh', 'tree_dbh_cm',
       'stump_diam', 'curb_loc', 'status', 'health', 'spc_latin', 'spc_common',
       'steward', 'guards', 'sidewalk', 'user_type', 'problems', 'root_stone',
       'root_grate', 'root_other', 'trunk_wire', 'trnk_light', 'trnk_other',
       'brch_light', 'brch_shoe', 'brch_other', 'address', 'postcode',
       'zip_city', 'community board', 'borocode', 'borough', 'cncldist',
       'st_assem', 'st_senate', 'nta', 'nta_name', 'boro_ct', 'state',
       'latitude', 'longitude', 'x_sp', 'y_sp', 'council district',
       'census tract', 'bin', 'bbl', 'has_a_problem'],
      dtype='str')
In [143]:
# I will select the columns more important 

columns_to_keep = [
    'tree_id',
    'tree_dbh_cm',
    'health',
    'spc_latin',
    'spc_common',
    'sidewalk',
    'user_type',
    'problems',
    'postcode',
    'borough',
    'latitude',
    'longitude'
]

df_clean = df_clean[columns_to_keep]

Visualization¶

In [147]:
# Let's see the top 10 Species 
#  
df_clean['spc_common'].value_counts().head(10).plot(
    kind='bar',
    figsize=(10,5),
    title='Top 10 Tree Species in NYC'
)
Out[147]:
<Axes: title={'center': 'Top 10 Tree Species in NYC'}, xlabel='spc_common'>
In [148]:
health_species = pd.crosstab(df_clean['spc_common'],df_clean['health'])

print(health_species)

health                  Fair  Good  Poor
spc_common                              
'Schubert' chokecherry   717  3929   242
American beech            38   214    21
American elm            1295  6412   268
American hophornbeam     158   866    57
American hornbeam        203  1221    93
...                      ...   ...   ...
weeping willow            51   218    13
white ash                166   897    58
white oak                263  1331    92
white pine                36   155    11
willow oak               360  2725    99

[132 rows x 3 columns]
In [149]:
health_species['Good_pct'] = (

    health_species['Good'] / health_species.sum(axis=1)

) * 100 

print(health_species)

spc_common

pitch pine         0     33     0  100.000000

In [151]:
best = health_species['Good_pct'].head(10)
worst = health_species['Good_pct'].tail(10)

combined = pd.concat([best, worst])

colors = (
    ['green'] * len(best) +
    ['red'] * len(best)
)

combined.plot( 
    kind='bar',
    figsize=(12,8),
    color = colors,
    title ='Tree Species. Best vs Worst health',
    xlabel = 'Good health Percentage'
)
Out[151]:
<Axes: title={'center': 'Tree Species. Best vs Worst health'}, xlabel='Good health Percentage'>

In [153]:
health_colors = {
    'Good' : 'green',
    'Fair': 'orange',
    'Poor':'red'
}
In [154]:
df_clean.shape
Out[154]:
(652166, 12)
In [155]:
# Filtering some rows will help to visibility, like simulating a zoom 

df_filter = df_clean[
    (df_clean['latitude'] > 40.5) &
    (df_clean['latitude'] < 41) &
    (df_clean['longitude'] > -74.5) &
    (df_clean['longitude'] < -73.5)
]
In [156]:
df_filter.shape
Out[156]:
(651951, 12)
In [157]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,12))

for h, color in health_colors.items():
    subset = df_filter[df_filter['health'] == h]
    plt.scatter(
        subset['longitude'],
        subset['latitude'],
        c=color,
        s=1,
        alpha=0.3,
        label=h
    )

plt.legend(fontsize=12,markerscale=5)
plt.title('NYC Trees by health')
plt.show()
In [158]:
Borough_health = pd.crosstab(
    df_clean['borough'],
    df_clean['health'],
    normalize='index'
) * 100
In [159]:
Borough_health['Good'].plot(
    kind='bar',
    figsize=(8,5),
    title='Percentage of Healthy Trees by Borough'
)
Out[159]:
<Axes: title={'center': 'Percentage of Healthy Trees by Borough'}, xlabel='borough'>

In [162]:
density = pd.DataFrame({
    'borough':[
        'Brooklyn',
        'Manhattan',
        'Queens',
        'Staten Island',
        'Bronx'
    ],
    'density': [
        15227,
        28872,
        8542,
        3327,
        13482
    ]
})
In [163]:
poor_pct = (
    pd.crosstab(df_clean['borough'], df_clean['health'],
                normalize='index') * 100
) ['Poor'].reset_index()
In [164]:
borough_analysis = density.merge(
    poor_pct,
    on = 'borough'
)
In [165]:
borough_analysis.plot (
    kind = 'scatter',
    x = 'density',
    y = 'Poor',
    figsize=(8,5),
    title='Population Density vs Poor Tree Health'
)
Out[165]:
<Axes: title={'center': 'Population Density vs Poor Tree Health'}, xlabel='density', ylabel='Poor'>
